divergences.decision: ADR-002 / deployment view: cluster metadata supports selectable ETCD or built-in Metastore mode, with `metastore_head_node` manually configuring the Worker-0 Metastore head and deployment validation ensuring that head is one of `worker_nodes` and has a corresponding `metastore_address` in the worker config.
divergences.detail: The new `dscli validate_config` command validates only `worker_nodes`, `worker_port`, `worker_config_path`, and `ssh_auth`. It ignores `metastore_head_node`, even though this is part of the generated cluster config and the deployment path validates it before starting workers. As a result, a cluster config for Metastore mode can be reported as valid by the new pre-deploy validator while `dscli up` later rejects it, which diverges from the MM's explicit ETCD-vs-Metastore deployment model and the purpose of catching deploy-blocking structural config problems up front.
divergences.fix: Extend `validate_config` to validate `metastore_head_node` when it is non-empty: require it to be present in `worker_nodes`, and either validate the referenced `worker_config_path` contains a non-empty `metastore_address` or explicitly document and implement why worker-config cross-validation is out of scope for this command. Add unit tests for valid and invalid Metastore-mode configs.
